The Vault v0.5.2 adds Graphify runtime updates from Settings and hardens the graph build pipeline.

Highlights:
- Graphify updates from Settings: the Extensions tab now checks PyPI for newer Graphify releases on every detect, shows installed vs latest, and offers a one-click "Update Graphify" that upgrades the managed runtime, logs the run, and queues rebuilds of previously built project graphs. Newer Graphify versions name graph communities after their most-connected node, so upgraded graphs replace generic "Community N" labels with real names.
- Update visibility for agents: the persisted update check is included in the `vault_graphify_status` MCP tool, so connected agents can see runtime drift without network access.
- Graphify version stamping: every build record and project state now stores the Graphify CLI version that produced it (previously always empty).
- Interrupted-build recovery: project graphs stuck in "building" or "queued" after an app quit or crash now self-heal on the next status read — back to stale when the last good graph survives, failed or missing otherwise — and the dangling build history row is closed.
- Update safety: updates refuse to run while a build is active, execute only the previewed commands with no shell interpolation, and PATH or developer-source runtimes get a clear explanation instead of guessed upgrade commands.

This build packages the desktop app and the bundled `vault-memory` MCP sidecar runtime. After installing, open The Vault and use Settings -> Client setup to connect Codex, Claude Desktop, or Claude Code.
